core_mechanism: a stochastic contagion/spreading process on a network, reduced to degree-based mean-field rate equations, with a critical threshold in the transmission rate separating a regime where the spread dies out from one where it reaches a finite fraction of nodes — a threshold that stays finite for homogeneous/random-graph topologies but vanishes for heavy-tailed (scale-free) degree distributions
brief_reason: The abstract derives mean-field equations for a stochastic spreading model and analyzes a critical threshold in the spreading rate below which the spread cannot propagate, a threshold that becomes vanishingly small on scale-free networks in the infinite-size limit.
